reasoning: As of late December 2025, Tesla's stock is trading near all-time highs above $480. Based on market history, TSLA has not dropped below $200 at any point in 2025 (its lowest point was around $268 in the spring). The Manifold market probability was just 2% on December 11, 2025, which perfectly reflects the fact that an unresolved drop below $200 has not occurred. With only ten days left in the year, TSLA would have to experience an unprecedented stock market mega-crash of ~58% to drop below $200. This is astronomically unlikely. Accounting for extreme tail risks over a ~10-day period (such as extreme geopolitical events or unprecedented company-specific fraud revelations), the probability is negligibly small. I submit 2% to conform to the system bounds.
